text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Amelia Shah  
Riverside Medical Centre  
48 Kingfisher Way, London SE10 8QF  

Dear Dr Amelia Shah,  

Daniel Morris, DOB: 22-07-1983, Hospital No: M912457 NHS No. 4082169753  
Flat 5, 14 Brook Terrace, London NW5 2HL 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy following left temporal lobe gliosis/post-concussive headaches/essential tremor/mild anxiety disorder/vitamin D insufficiency. Currently living independently, supported by community epilepsy nurse. Non-smoker, minimal alcohol.  

Medication: Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
Lacosamide 100 mg twice daily.  
Cholecalciferol 1000 units daily.  
Sertraline 50 mg each morning.  

I reviewed Daniel Morris in clinic today, accompanied by his partner. He reports improved sleep regularity with the use of a consistent bedtime routine and reduced caffeine intake. Headaches have been less frequent and respond to simple analgesia. He remains in part-time employment and has resumed cycling on weekends, avoiding sleep deprivation and alcohol excess. No recent hospital attendances. 

Seizures: The patient has focal epilepsy. He describes Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode. Specifically, approximately every three months he experiences a brief cluster of focal aware auras (rising epigastric sensation and déjà vu lasting 10–20 seconds over 1–2 days) culminating in a single generalised convulsion at the end of the cluster, with tongue-biting on two occasions and post-ictal fatigue lasting up to 24 hours. There have been no additional convulsions outside these quarterly clusters. He and his partner keep a digital seizure diary which corroborates this pattern since January 2024. No witnessed focal impaired-awareness seizures between clusters. No clear triggers identified beyond occasional poor sleep preceding the cluster. 

Examination: Alert and oriented. Speech fluent. Cranial nerves intact. No focal motor deficit; mild symmetric postural tremor of the hands. Gait normal. Weight stable. Blood pressure 126/78 mmHg. 

Investigations: Routine bloods in July 2025 showed normal FBC, U&E, LFTs; vitamin D borderline low at 23 ng/mL (supplementation ongoing). Lamotrigine level within therapeutic range. MRI brain (March 2024) unchanged focal gliotic scarring in left mesial temporal region; no new lesions. 

Plan:  
- Continue lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and lacosamide 100 mg twice daily without change.  
- Reinforce adherence and sleep hygiene; continue digital seizure diary.  
- Provide updated individualised seizure action plan to the patient and partner (copy to community epilepsy nurse).  
- Safety: continue driving cessation as per DVLA guidance given ongoing convulsions; patient understands and agrees.  
- Blood tests (U&E, LFT, vitamin D) to be repeated in primary care in three months; we will review results via shared record.  
- Consider ambulatory EEG if cluster pattern changes or if intercurrent focal impaired-awareness events emerge.  
- Follow-up in six months, earlier if frequency increases or injuries occur. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode. Specifically, approximately every three months he experiences a brief cluster ... culminating in a single generalised convulsion at the end of the cluster
Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly describes a recurring pattern: "Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode." It further clarifies that approximately every three months he has a brief cluster of focal aware auras over 1–2 days culminating in a single generalised convulsion, with no additional convulsions outside these quarterly clusters and no focal impaired-awareness seizures between clusters. Therefore, the seizure frequency should be represented as clusters per time with number per cluster. Quarterly = every three months = 1 cluster per 3 months, with 1 convulsion per cluster. According to the rules, we select the highest frequency among types; the cluster frequency is the governing pattern, and the convulsion count per cluster is 1. The allowed format is '<value1> cluster per <value2> <unit>, <value3> per cluster'.
seizure_frequency_number: Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode. Specifically, approximately every three months he experiences a brief cluster ... culminating in a single generalised convulsion at the end of the cluster
reference: Quarterly clusters with one convulsions per episode
